parsed_review.summary: CP00-887 (RP29 Commercial Readiness permission-matrix boundary tail, risk B, 40 units, RP29.P06.M04.S11-RP29.P06.M06.S06) passes read-only review. Verified by source inspection (Read/Grep/Glob only; no Bash, no writes): pack identity, risk class, unit count, range, deliverable distribution {ui:8,claude_review:2,security_audit:9,test:8,implementation:13}, and micro-title distribution {Secondary Workflow Slice:12,Permission And Audit Binding:22,Synthetic Fixture Set:6} match across registry.js binding, manifest plan_binding_snapshot, closeout-pack-plan.json, the readiness contract, and the prompt baseline. Section rows resolve correctly (M04 slice(10,22)=12, M05=22, M06 slice(0,6)=6). The CP887 descriptor/case-set is genuinely descriptor-only: createPermissionMatrixSectionRows sets descriptor_only:true with every runtime/write/leak/bypass flag false, and validateCommercialCp887PermissionMatrixBoundaryTailDescriptor enforces permission_matrix_descriptor, no legal-hold/ethical-wall/object-ACL bypass, security-trimming/leak-prevention omit unauthorized data, deny-over-allow enforced, no cross-tenant exposure, no real data or secrets. Authority boundary holds (claude_is_final_approval:false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, runtime opening deferred to CP00-888 with human approval). Hermes packet emits_runtime_receipt:false at gate H29 (deterministic review/evidence only, not product runtime); Claude packet read_only:true and promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false. The readiness contract (validation.valid:true, latest_pack CP00-887, projection CommercialCp887...) and compatibility alias are consistent; the deterministic validation script and model.test.js assertions wire binding chain, coverage, descriptor(errors==[]), and contract projection; index.js re-exports all referenced symbols. Changed-file scope is respected and unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room) are correctly excluded. Review-evidence hardening: current claude-review-result.json is the pending placeholder (exactly_one_valid_pack_level_claude_review_run:false, no invalid attempts), not a completed review with 0-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-shaped output accepted as valid; readiness auth preflight passed (exit 0, valid JSON). The pack correctly reports production_ready:false / status in_progress / verdict PENDING_REVIEW and does not prematurely claim production or enterprise trust. No P0/P1 findings. Two P3 process notes recorded below; neither blocks this CP closeout. Note: as a read-only reviewer I inspected source and recorded evidence but did not execute any validation commands; the PASS is contingent on the full deterministic ladder passing post-review.

findings.title: Full deterministic validation ladder deferred to post-review (only targeted checks recorded)
findings.details: command-evidence.json records only targeted validation as passing (commercial_syntax_check_initial, commercial_model_service_tests_initial, rp29_commercial_validate_initial — all exit_code 0) and explicitly notes the full deterministic validation ladder remains required after review normalization, adjudication update, and queue regeneration. The expected pre-closeout commands (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) are not yet evidenced. As a read-only reviewer (Read/Grep/Glob only, no Bash) I cannot execute them; source inspection shows the implementation is internally consistent and the pack-specific rp29:commercial validation already passed, but full-ladder PASS is unverified at review time.
findings.recommendation: Run the full deterministic validation ladder after normalizing this review receipt and before commit/closeout; capture the ladder evidence (exit codes / git diff --check clean) in the pack before promoting production_ready.

findings.title: Pack-level Claude review receipt is still the pending placeholder
findings.details: manifest.pack_level_claude_review.status is pending_review with review_receipt_ref null and valid_review_receipts empty; claude-review-result.json carries the PENDING_REVIEW placeholder (raw_output.stdout_bytes 0, exactly_one_valid_pack_level_claude_review_run:false) and construction-inspection.json/adjudication.md report PENDING_REVIEW with production_ready:false. This is the correct gated state, not a defect — the 0-byte placeholder is the slot awaiting this review's normalized output, not a completed review being accepted as valid. Confirms the production_ready gate is closed pending exactly one valid normalized receipt plus the validation ladder.
findings.recommendation: Normalize exactly one valid receipt from this review, update adjudication.md and construction-inspection.json accordingly, then proceed to the deterministic ladder; do not promote production_ready until receipt + ladder are complete.
